_comment: Audited inventory of native NotificationRouter *producer* call sites -- the code paths that
emit a bottom-right notification instead of a transient modal message box (docs/design/
07-feedback.md 7.5). It is a source-implementation ledger only: it does not claim a native
build, notification pixels, or runtime interaction. Each producer is verified fail-closed
against real (comment-stripped) source by bin/check-notification-producer-contract.py and its
mutation suite bin/test_notification_producer_contract.py.
Scope: this contract validates ONLY the registered producers and the shared router seam. It
deliberately does NOT try to prove that every legacy dialog has been routed -- that unrouted
backlog is tracked by dialog-notification-policy.csv. It fails on an unreal producer (missing
file, function, router call, or source literal) or a mislabeled one (wrong severity, a source
outside the SafeDisplayText allowlist, or informational_only cleared).
A producer may also declare an optional 'wiring_markers' list of {file, pattern, note} entries.
The field is optional -- producers without it (the Batch-A viewprn/newhelp notices) stay valid --
but when present the checker requires every anchored pattern to exist as real (comment-stripped)
source. That binds REACHABILITY, not just existence: a partial revert that removes only a
producer's arming/consumption/guard site leaves the producer function defined but as dead code,
which the existence checks alone would still pass; the wiring markers fail closed on it instead.
Every producer routes purely informational text (a status/acknowledgement), so it is never a
modal input/destructive/credential/security prompt: informational_only is invariably true and
the router's Classify keeps the informational case non-modal. Display text is fixed, translated
and carries no document data, so it uses NotificationPrivacy::SafeDisplayText under an approved
source; the store redacts anything else regardless of this hint.
Cross-module diversity: 'min_producer_modules' requires the registered producers to span at least
that many distinct owning modules (the first path segment of each producer file). It is the
checkable evidence that observable-feedback routing is a suite-wide convention rather than a
single-file trick: if the registry ever collapses back toward one module the rule fails closed.
The value is pinned at the exact current diversity (sfx2/sw/svx = 3) with zero slack, so removing
or renaming a producer down to fewer modules must be a deliberate, reviewed change to this number,
never a silent regression.

producers.purpose: Printer-busy notice: the one-way abort is reported to the bottom-right stack instead of a modal OK box.
Help-viewer 'no matches' notice: routed to the stack while the help search dialog stays interactive.

First transient action-confirmation: mirrors the Find & Replace 'Replace All' outcome (replacement count / no-match) into the bottom-right stack; Success for a completed replacement, Information for no match.

wiring_markers.note: Arms the one-shot Replace All confirmation flag in CommandHdl_Impl before the synchronous FID_SEARCH_NOW dispatch. A partial revert removing only this arming leaves the producer defined but never armed -- dead code the existence checks alone would still pass.

Consumes the armed flag by calling the producer in the SvxSearchDialogWrapper::SetSearchLabel outcome sink (both the SearchLabel and OUString overloads). Removing the call sites leaves the producer function unreachable while its definition survives.
Material theme opt-in guard read by lcl_IsMaterialFeedbackActive; under any other VCL_FILE_WIDGET_THEME value the whole confirmation path is a no-op, so this literal must remain live.

producers.purpose: No working mail client on 'Send as email': the one-way CANCELLED notice routes to the bottom-right stack instead of the modal ErrorFindEmailDialog OK box; sfx2-module producer, the same module as the router.

wiring_markers.note: The converted notice routes the audited primary string in the !bSend branch of SfxMailModel::Send. A revert to the modal ErrorFindEmailDialog removes this reference, leaving the producer function defined but the notification dead.

producers.purpose: Writer HTML/Basic source-view Find 'search key not found': routed to the bottom-right stack instead of the modal InfoNotFoundDialog OK box -- a distinct sw-module feature, sibling of the sfx2 newhelp no-search-text notice.

wiring_markers.note: The converted notice routes the audited string in the bNotFoundMessage branch of StartSearchAndReplace. Reverting to the modal InfoNotFoundDialog removes this reference, leaving the producer function defined but the notification dead.

producers.purpose: Label Format 'predefined label cannot be overwritten': routed to the bottom-right stack instead of the modal CannotSaveLabelDialog OK box while the Label Format dialog itself stays open. NOTE: the enclosing-function anchor is a bare IMPL_LINK_NOARG macro invocation, not a Class::Method definition -- a deliberate deviation from the other producers' anchor style, flagged for reviewer sign-off.

wiring_markers.note: The converted notice routes the audited primary string in the IsPredefinedLabel branch of the OkHdl handler. Reverting to the modal CannotSaveLabelDialog removes this reference, leaving the handler defined but the notification dead.

producers.purpose: Read-only-content notice (edit attempt on write-protected content): both the former sync and async InfoReadonlyDialog paths now route one non-blocking notice to the bottom-right stack, keeping the conditional folded-outline text variant. Paired unit with textfld-readonly-content-notice (same InfoReadonlyDialog .ui).

wiring_markers.note: The converted notice routes the audited default primary string in InfoReadOnlyDialog. Reverting to the modal InfoReadonlyDialog removes this reference, leaving the producer function defined but the notification dead.

producers.purpose: Read-only-content notice on a field-insert request into a write-protected selection: the async InfoReadonlyDialog inside SwTextShell::ExecField is routed to the bottom-right stack (plain-text variant, no folded case). Paired unit with wrtsh1-readonly-content-notice (same InfoReadonlyDialog .ui).

wiring_markers.note: The converted notice routes the audited default primary string in the HasReadonlySel branch of ExecField. Reverting to the modal InfoReadonlyDialog removes this reference, leaving the producer's routing dead.
